title: R package development
text: Always use = assignment, never <-. 2-space indentation, 120-character line limit. snake_case functions variables, CamelCase R6 classes. calling function imported package foo write foo::bar() bar() Double quotes strings, explicit TRUE/FALSE (never T/F), explicit 1L integers. Use implicit return values functions. Prefer result = (...) ... else ... (...) { result = ... } else { result = ... } difference branches assigned value. User-facing API (exported functions, public R6 methods) must checkmate assert_*() argument checks. internal code, match existing level defensiveness. Use mlr3misc utilities appropriate: map(), map_chr(), invoke(), calculate_hash(), str_collapse(), %nin%, %??%. implementing something, read similar existing files first match established patterns. Always use #nolint next disable linters next line instead # nolint line.

title: R package development
text: Name file important contained function / class whitespaces, special chart filenames Usually one large function / class, per file, adding multiple smaller helpers ok

title: R package development
text: Derived classes must declare #' @include ParentClass.R roxygen header. controls Collate: field DESCRIPTION base classes load derived classes.

title: R package development
text: Use checkmate arg-checks Use data.table efficient table structures OOP-stype use R6 Use cli format messages, warnings, errors prints

title: R package development
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test_{name}.R. new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests. Strive keep tests minimal comments. full test suite takes long time. run tests relevant changes devtools::test(filter = '^{name}').

title: R package development
text: Every user-facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Wrap roxygen comments 120 characters. Write one sentence per line. sentence exceeds limit, break comma appropriate point. Internal functions roxygen documentation. Always re-document package changing roxygen2 comment. Don’t hand-edit generated artifacts: man/, NAMESPACE. Roxygen templates live man-roxygen/ Bibliographic references go R/bibentries.R cited `r format_bib("key")`.

title: R package development
text: Every user-facing change given bullet NEWS.md. add bullets small documentation changes internal refactorings. bullet briefly describe change end user mention related issue parentheses. bullet can consist multiple sentences contain new lines (.e. line wrap). change related function, put name function early bullet. Order bullets alphabetically function name. Put bullets don’t mention function names beginning.

title: R package development
text: use gh retrieve information issue, always use --comments read comments.

title: R package development
text: following applies natural language text, docs, commments, NEWS, etc, code Use American english Use Oxford comma capitalize normal nouns method names. “Bayesian” capitalized, “random forest” . Use cspell check typos, add needed words .cspell/project-words.txt reasonable

title: Black-Box Optimization Toolkit
text: Package website: release | dev bbotk black-box optimization framework R. features highly configurable search spaces via paradox package optimizes every user-defined objective function. package includes several optimization algorithms e.g. Random Search, Grid Search, Iterated Racing, Bayesian Optimization (mlr3mbo) Hyperband (mlr3hyperband). bbotk base package mlr3tuning, mlr3fselect miesmuschel.

title: bbotk: Black-Box Optimization Toolkit — bbotk-package
text: Features highly configurable search spaces via 'paradox' package optimizes every user-defined objective function. package includes several optimization algorithms e.g. Random Search, Iterated Racing, Bayesian Optimization ('mlr3mbo') Hyperband ('mlr3hyperband'). bbotk base package 'mlr3tuning', 'mlr3fselect' 'miesmuschel'.

title: Assertion for bbotk objects — bbotk_assertions
text: assertion functions ensure right class attribute, optionally additional properties. Additionally, following compound assertions implemented: assert_terminable(terminator, instance) (Terminator, OptimInstance) -> NULL Checks terminator applicable optimization. assert_instance_properties(optimizer, instance) (Optimizer, OptimInstance) -> NULL Checks instance applicable optimizer. assertion fails, exception raised. Otherwise, input object returned invisibly.

text: objective (function(xdt)) Objective optimize. first arg (name 'xdt' enforced) data.table (scalar) columns corresponding exactly search space, order. function must return numeric vector exactly length number rows dt, containing objective values. search_space (paradox::ParamSet) Search space decision variables. Must non-empty, can contain p_int, p_dbl, p_fct, p_lgl, must bounded. control (local_search_control) Control parameters local search, generated local_search_control(). init_points (data.table) Initial points start local search , format described argument 'objective'. title: Local Search — local_search
text: (named list). List elements: 'x': (list) best point found, length element names order correspond exactly search space. 'y': (numeric(1)) objective value best point.

title: Local Search — local_search
text: run "n_searches" parallel. search runs "n_steps" iterations. search every iteration generate "n_neighs" neighbors. neighbor current point, exactly one parameter mutated. Mutation works like : num params: scale 0,1, add Gaussian noise sd "mut_sd", scale back. clip lower upper bounds. int params: numeric parameters, round end. factor params: sample new level unused levels parameter. logical params: flip bit. Hierarchical dependencies handled like : active params can mutated. mutation happened, check conditions search space topological order. condition met, set param NA (making inactive); conditions met param, currently NA, set random valid value. neighbors generated, evaluate . go best neighbor, stay current point best neighbor worse. restart mechanism avoid local minima. search, keep track number -improvement steps. number exceeds "stagnate_max", restart search random point.

Run Optimizers Sequentially — mlr_optimizers_chain
text: OptimizerBatchChain allows run multiple OptimizerBatch sequentially. OptimizerBatch (optional) additional Terminator can specified construction. original Terminator OptimInstanceBatch guards optimization process whole, additional Terminators guard individual OptimizerBatch. optimization process works follows: first OptimizerBatch run OptimInstanceBatch relying TerminatorCombo original Terminator OptimInstanceBatch (optional) additional Terminator passed construction. TerminatorCombo indicates termination (usually via additional Terminator), second OptimizerBatch run. continues optimizers unless original Terminator OptimInstanceBatch indicates termination. OptimizerBatchChain can also used random restarts Optimizer (applicable) setting Terminator OptimInstanceBatch TerminatorNone setting identical additional Terminators construction.

title: Non-linear Optimization — mlr_optimizers_nloptr
text: OptimizerBatchNLoptr class implements non-linear optimization. Calls nloptr::nloptr() package nloptr.

title: Non-linear Optimization — mlr_optimizers_nloptr
text: Johnson, G S (2020). “NLopt nonlinear-optimization package.” https://github.com/stevengj/nlopt.

title: Non-linear Optimization — mlr_optimizers_nloptr
text: algorithm character(1) Algorithm use. See nloptr::nloptr.print.options() available algorithms. x0 numeric() Initial parameter values. Use start_values parameter create "random" "center" start values. start_values character(1) Create "random" start values based "center" search space? latter case, center parameters trafo applied. Custom start values can passed via x0 parameter. approximate_eval_grad_f logical(1) gradients numerically approximated via finite differences (nloptr::nl.grad). required certain algorithms. Note function evaluations required numerical gradient approximation logged usual treated differently regular function evaluations , e.g., Terminators. meaning control parameters, see nloptr::nloptr() nloptr::nloptr.print.options().

title: Non-linear Optimization — mlr_optimizers_nloptr
text: algorithm can terminated Terminators. Additionally, following internal termination parameters can used: stopval numeric(1) Stop value. Deactivate -Inf. Default -Inf. maxtime integer(1) Maximum time. Deactivate -1L. Default -1L. maxeval integer(1) Maximum number evaluations. Deactivate -1L. Default -1L. xtol_rel numeric(1) Relative tolerance. Original default 10^-4. Deactivate -1. Overwritten -1. xtol_abs numeric(1) Absolute tolerance. Deactivate -1. Default -1. ftol_rel numeric(1) Relative tolerance. Deactivate -1. Default -1. ftol_abs numeric(1) Absolute tolerance. Deactivate -1. Default -1.

title: Optimization via Random Search — mlr_optimizers_random_search

title: bbotk 1.10.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-08 chore: Minimum required version rush now 1.0.0. Removed compatibility workarounds older versions. fix: Remove non-API C entry points caused CRAN warnings (#332). feat: Add mlr_test_functions dictionary well-known 2-D optimization test functions (Branin, Rosenbrock, Himmelblau, Rastrigin, etc.) sugar functions otfun() / otfuns().

title: bbotk 1.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-28 feat: Allow "learn" tag (direction=0) alongside minimize/maximize Codomain. feat: Add new base class EvalInstance OptimInstance now inherits. EvalInstance keeps information process evaluates Objective necessarily optimizing . feat: Exporting formerly internal choose_search_space() function. feat: Use mlr3misc error classes errors warnings. compatibility: Compatible rush 1.0.0. fix: Terminator print method works correctly now.
